item.title: I benchmarked two ways to handle concurrency. One was 13x faster.
item.description: Channels feel cleaner. Mutexes are faster for reads.

item.title: Don't share memory to communicate. Communicate to share memory.
item.description: Go has a different answer to concurrency than most languages.

item.title: I crashed my server on purpose to understand why
item.description: What happens when two goroutines write to the same map at the same time.

item.title: TCP gives you bytes. RESP gives you meaning.
item.description: Why raw bytes are not enough, and how Redis solved message framing with a dead simple protocol.

item.title: How Redis handles a million connections without breaking a sweat
item.description: Redis is single-threaded. It should be slow. It is not. Here is why.

item.title: My first week of Go: building a tiny TCP server
item.description: How I built a TCP server in Go from scratch and what actually made things click.

item.title: Hello, World (from a sleeping cat)
item.description: Spinning up a cutesy retro dev blog that lives entirely on GitHub Pages.
